title: Shri Kaleshwar Mandir
tagline: Shri Kaleshwar Mandir — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: Shri Kaleshwar Mandir, Shree Kaleshwara Mandira (according to Marathi phonology § Vowels) or Shree Kaleshwara Devasthana (According to natives of Nerur village ) is a large Hindu t
overview.description: Shri Kaleshwar Mandir, Shree Kaleshwara Mandira (according to Marathi phonology § Vowels) or Shree Kaleshwara Devasthana (According to natives of Nerur village ) is a large Hindu temple in Nerur, Sindhudurg district in Maharashtra, India. It is dedicated to the village deity, Shree Kaleshwara, who is also called Kaloba. The temple dates back 700 to 800 years, when, according to legend, a brahmin dug into the ground and discovered a natural lingam, an emblem of Shiva. The temple was founded on that spot, and the lingam is now enshrined in the temple's sanctum sanctorum.

topPlaces.name: Nerur, Maharashtra
topPlaces.description: Nerur is a town of Kudal Taluka of Sindhudurg district (Sindhudurga in native Malvani language) of Maharashtra in India. It is one of the smaller towns in the district having 32 wadis.

topPlaces.name: Sarambal
topPlaces.description: Sarambal is an ancient village located in Kudal taluka. Although Sarambal is predominantly Hindu and Muslim, there are several Christian societies located near Kavilkata (Kavil Gao) village.

topPlaces.name: Mandakuli
topPlaces.description: Mandakuli is a small village in Kudal in Sindhudurg district, Maharashtra, India. It is situated on Karli River in southwest Maharashtara.

topPlaces.name: Dhamapur Dam
topPlaces.description: Dhamapur Dam is an earth-fill dam constructed in 1530 by the villagers and Nagesh Desai who was Mandlik of the Vijaynagar dynasty. The lake behind the dam is one of the biggest lakes of Sindhudurg district in the state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Pendur
topPlaces.description: Pendur village is located in Malvan Tehsil of Sindhudurg district in Maharashtra, India. It is situated 26 km away from sub-district headquarter Malwan and 12 km away from district headquarter Oras.

items.note: Nerur is a town of Kudal Taluka of Sindhudurg district (Sindhudurga in native Malvani language) of Maharashtra in India. It is one of the smaller towns in the district having 32 wadis.
Sarambal is an ancient village located in Kudal taluka. Although Sarambal is predominantly Hindu and Muslim, there are several Christian societies located near Kavilkata (Kavil Gao) village.
Mandakuli is a small village in Kudal in Sindhudurg district, Maharashtra, India. It is situated on Karli River in southwest Maharashtara.

Dhamapur Dam is an earth-fill dam constructed in 1530 by the villagers and Nagesh Desai who was Mandlik of the Vijaynagar dynasty. The lake behind the dam is one of the biggest lakes of Sindhudurg district in the state of Maharashtra in India.
Pendur village is located in Malvan Tehsil of Sindhudurg district in Maharashtra, India. It is situated 26 km away from sub-district headquarter Malwan and 12 km away from district headquarter Oras.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shri Kaleshwar Mandir?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shri Kaleshwar Mandir is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shri Kaleshwar Mandir?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Manohar International Airport, Mopa (North Goa) (38 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (70 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shri Kaleshwar Mandir?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shri Kaleshwar Mandir at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shri Kaleshwar Mandir?
faq.a: Stays in Shri Kaleshwar Mandir start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shri Kaleshwar Mandir?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nerur, Maharashtra, Sarambal, Mandakuli, Dhamapur Dam, Pendur.
